forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,58 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '80'
+title: 'AI composer: slash-command autocomplete from ACP `availableCommands`'
+---
+## What
+
+The AI panel's prompt composer (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-prompt-composer.tsx`) currently has **no autocomplete** â€” the composer's docstring (the "Why we still hand-roll the Enter-submit binding" section) calls this out and warns that any future maintainer adding autocomplete must re-introduce the `completionStatus` Enter-yield guard. Today, when an ACP agent advertises slash commands via `session/update`'s `available_commands_update` (e.g. `/plan`, `/review`, `/finish` from this very board's skills), the user can type `/` in the composer but gets no completion menu â€” they have to remember every command by hand.
+
+We already have a smart shared mention-autocomplete component that powers `#tag`, `@actor`, and `^task` completions in the filter editor and the markdown field editor: `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/cm-mention-autocomplete.ts` (parameterized by prefix character) and `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/hooks/use-mention-extensions.ts` (the React hook that wires it to CodeMirror). The job is to **reuse that same shared component for `/` slash-command completions in the composer**, fed by the live ACP `availableCommands` list â€” not to special-case slash handling in the composer.
+
+### Approach
+
+1. **Extend the shared mention infrastructure to support a slash-command flavor**, in `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/cm-mention-autocomplete.ts`. The existing `createMentionCompletionSource(prefix, search)` already accepts any prefix string and a sync or async search function â€” slash commands are just `prefix = "/"` with a sync search over the live `availableCommands` array. The pieces that **don't** transfer cleanly are the entity-shaped `MentionSearchResult` (`{ slug, displayName, color }`) and the colored-dot `info()` widget â€” commands carry a `description` instead of a color. Add a second result shape or extend the existing one so a command result can supply a `description` and skip the color dot. Keep one shared source builder; do not fork into a parallel implementation.
+
+2. **Add a `useCommandCompletionExtension` hook** (or extend the existing `useMentionExtensions` hook in `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/hooks/use-mention-extensions.ts`) that takes an `AvailableCommand[]` (from `@agentclientprotocol/sdk`) and returns the CM6 extension assembled via the same shared `createMentionAutocomplete(sources)` helper. The hook lives in `hooks/` so it has the right React boundary, but the heavy lifting stays in `lib/cm-mention-autocomplete.ts`.
+
+3. **Wire the new extension into the composer.** `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-prompt-composer.tsx`:
+   - Accept the `availableCommands: AvailableCommand[]` list as a new prop on `AiPromptComposerProps` (defaulting to `[]` so existing tests stay green).
+   - Build the autocomplete extension via the new hook and append it to `baseExtensions`.
+   - **Re-introduce the autocomplete-yield guard on the Enter-submit binding** that the composer's docstring already warns about: import `completionStatus` from `@codemirror/autocomplete` and have `buildEnterSubmitExtension`'s `run` return `false` when `completionStatus(view.state) === "active"`, so Enter accepts the highlighted completion instead of submitting. Update the docstring to reflect that autocomplete is now present.
+
+4. **Thread `availableCommands` from the conversation to the composer.** In `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-panel.tsx`, `AiPanelConversation` already destructures the live `useConversation` result; pull `state.availableCommands` and pass it down through `ComposerArea` to `AiPromptComposer`. `ComposerArea` is in the same file â€” add the prop pass-through there too.
+
+### Non-goals
+
+- Do **not** intercept slash commands locally or invoke them through any client-side router. Submitting `/plan` should still send the literal text `/plan` to the agent via `sendPrompt`, exactly as it does today â€” the agent owns command execution. This task is purely about discoverability via autocomplete.
+- Do **not** add a color dot, color picker, or any other mention-specific chrome to command completions. The completion menu for `/` should show the command name and its description; the existing `info()` callback shape is enough to render a description without a color dot.
+- Do not change the `#`/`@`/`^` mention sources or their behavior.
+
+### Why this fits the existing pattern
+
+`createMentionCompletionSource` is already prefix-parameterized and accepts a sync search function (the `MentionSearchSync` type) â€” the slash-command source is the smallest possible extension of the existing component, not a new component. `createMentionAutocomplete(sources)` already collapses multiple sources into one `autocompletion()` call to avoid the "Config merge conflict for field override" error, so adding a `/` source to the same array stays one autocompletion extension. Reusing the shared component is what keeps the composer's editor, the filter editor, and the markdown field editor on one CM6 autocomplete primitive.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] Typing `/` (anywhere in the composer) opens a completion menu listing every entry in the current `availableCommands` from the live ACP conversation; typing additional characters filters by name prefix/substring (mirroring the existing mention autocomplete filter behavior).
+- [ ] Each completion shows the command `name` (with the `/` prefix) and its `description` in the info area. No color dot is rendered for slash completions.
+- [ ] Accepting a completion (Enter or click) inserts the literal text `/<name>` into the buffer. The composer does NOT intercept or transform the command â€” `sendPrompt` is called with the literal text on the next plain Enter.
+- [ ] When the completion menu is open, plain Enter accepts the highlighted completion and does NOT submit the buffer. With the menu closed, plain Enter still submits per the existing policy. Shift-Enter still inserts a newline regardless of menu state.
+- [ ] Escape with the menu open closes the menu (CM6 default); Escape with the menu closed still drills out via the existing `buildSubmitCancelExtensions` path.
+- [ ] No regressions to the filter editor or markdown field editor: `#tag`, `@actor`, and `^task` completions still work identically there.
+- [ ] When `availableCommands` is empty (a model that advertises no commands, or before the first `available_commands_update`), typing `/` produces no menu and Enter submits normally.
+- [ ] The shared `createMentionCompletionSource` / `createMentionAutocomplete` helpers remain the single completion-source assembly point in `lib/cm-mention-autocomplete.ts`; slash support is added by extending result-shape handling, not by forking into a parallel `cm-slash-autocomplete.ts`.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] Extend `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/cm-mention-autocomplete.test.ts` with cases for the `/` prefix: an empty `availableCommands` array yields `null`, a populated one yields a `CompletionResult` whose options' `label` is `/${name}`, `apply` is `/${name}`, and `info()` renders the description text. Run `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test cm-mention-autocomplete` and expect green.
+- [ ] Add a unit test in `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/hooks/__tests__/use-mention-extensions.test.ts` (or a sibling test file for the new hook) that mounts the hook with a fixture `AvailableCommand[]` and asserts the returned extension array contains a single `autocompletion` config wired to the `/` source plus the existing mention sources.
+- [ ] Extend `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-prompt-composer.test.tsx` with: (a) typing `/` opens the menu when `availableCommands` is non-empty and does not when it is empty; (b) plain Enter with the menu open does NOT call `onSend` and instead accepts the highlighted completion (assert the buffer contents); (c) plain Enter with the menu closed still calls `onSend`; (d) Shift-Enter always inserts a newline. Run `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test ai-prompt-composer` and expect green.
+- [ ] Add an `ai-panel` integration test (extend `ai-panel.test.tsx` or `ai-panel-container.test.tsx`) that pushes a fake `available_commands_update` through the conversation store and asserts the composer's autocomplete fires for `/`. Run `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test ai-panel` and expect green.
+- [ ] Full UI suite: `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test` runs green.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` â€” write failing tests first (the autocomplete-yield behavior on Enter is the most important regression guard), then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

-## What
-
-The AI panel's prompt composer (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-prompt-composer.tsx`) currently has **no autocomplete** â€” the composer's docstring (the "Why we still hand-roll the Enter-submit binding" section) calls this out and warns that any future maintainer adding autocomplete must re-introduce the `completionStatus` Enter-yield guard. Today, when an ACP agent advertises slash commands via `session/update`'s `available_commands_update` (e.g. `/plan`, `/review`, `/finish` from this very board's skills), the user can type `/` in the composer but gets no completion menu â€” they have to remember every command by hand.
-
-We already have a smart shared mention-autocomplete component that powers `#tag`, `@actor`, and `^task` completions in the filter editor and the markdown field editor: `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/cm-mention-autocomplete.ts` (parameterized by prefix character) and `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/hooks/use-mention-extensions.ts` (the React hook that wires it to CodeMirror). The job is to **reuse that same shared component for `/` slash-command completions in the composer**, fed by the live ACP `availableCommands` list â€” not to special-case slash handling in the composer.
-
-### Approach
-
-1. **Extend the shared mention infrastructure to support a slash-command flavor**, in `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/cm-mention-autocomplete.ts`. The existing `createMentionCompletionSource(prefix, search)` already accepts any prefix string and a sync or async search function â€” slash commands are just `prefix = "/"` with a sync search over the live `availableCommands` array. The pieces that **don't** transfer cleanly are the entity-shaped `MentionSearchResult` (`{ slug, displayName, color }`) and the colored-dot `info()` widget â€” commands carry a `description` instead of a color. Add a second result shape or extend the existing one so a command result can supply a `description` and skip the color dot. Keep one shared source builder; do not fork into a parallel implementation.
-
-2. **Add a `useCommandCompletionExtension` hook** (or extend the existing `useMentionExtensions` hook in `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/hooks/use-mention-extensions.ts`) that takes an `AvailableCommand[]` (from `@agentclientprotocol/sdk`) and returns the CM6 extension assembled via the same shared `createMentionAutocomplete(sources)` helper. The hook lives in `hooks/` so it has the right React boundary, but the heavy lifting stays in `lib/cm-mention-autocomplete.ts`.
-
-3. **Wire the new extension into the composer.** `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-prompt-composer.tsx`:
-   - Accept the `availableCommands: AvailableCommand[]` list as a new prop on `AiPromptComposerProps` (defaulting to `[]` so existing tests stay green).
-   - Build the autocomplete extension via the new hook and append it to `baseExtensions`.
-   - **Re-introduce the autocomplete-yield guard on the Enter-submit binding** that the composer's docstring already warns about: import `completionStatus` from `@codemirror/autocomplete` and have `buildEnterSubmitExtension`'s `run` return `false` when `completionStatus(view.state) === "active"`, so Enter accepts the highlighted completion instead of submitting. Update the docstring to reflect that autocomplete is now present.
-
-4. **Thread `availableCommands` from the conversation to the composer.** In `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-panel.tsx`, `AiPanelConversation` already destructures the live `useConversation` result; pull `state.availableCommands` and pass it down through `ComposerArea` to `AiPromptComposer`. `ComposerArea` is in the same file â€” add the prop pass-through there too.
-
-### Non-goals
-
-- Do **not** intercept slash commands locally or invoke them through any client-side router. Submitting `/plan` should still send the literal text `/plan` to the agent via `sendPrompt`, exactly as it does today â€” the agent owns command execution. This task is purely about discoverability via autocomplete.
-- Do **not** add a color dot, color picker, or any other mention-specific chrome to command completions. The completion menu for `/` should show the command name and its description; the existing `info()` callback shape is enough to render a description without a color dot.
-- Do not change the `#`/`@`/`^` mention sources or their behavior.
-
-### Why this fits the existing pattern
-
-`createMentionCompletionSource` is already prefix-parameterized and accepts a sync search function (the `MentionSearchSync` type) â€” the slash-command source is the smallest possible extension of the existing component, not a new component. `createMentionAutocomplete(sources)` already collapses multiple sources into one `autocompletion()` call to avoid the "Config merge conflict for field override" error, so adding a `/` source to the same array stays one autocompletion extension. Reusing the shared component is what keeps the composer's editor, the filter editor, and the markdown field editor on one CM6 autocomplete primitive.
-
-## Acceptance Criteria
-
-- [ ] Typing `/` (anywhere in the composer) opens a completion menu listing every entry in the current `availableCommands` from the live ACP conversation; typing additional characters filters by name prefix/substring (mirroring the existing mention autocomplete filter behavior).
-- [ ] Each completion shows the command `name` (with the `/` prefix) and its `description` in the info area. No color dot is rendered for slash completions.
-- [ ] Accepting a completion (Enter or click) inserts the literal text `/<name>` into the buffer. The composer does NOT intercept or transform the command â€” `sendPrompt` is called with the literal text on the next plain Enter.
-- [ ] When the completion menu is open, plain Enter accepts the highlighted completion and does NOT submit the buffer. With the menu closed, plain Enter still submits per the existing policy. Shift-Enter still inserts a newline regardless of menu state.
-- [ ] Escape with the menu open closes the menu (CM6 default); Escape with the menu closed still drills out via the existing `buildSubmitCancelExtensions` path.
-- [ ] No regressions to the filter editor or markdown field editor: `#tag`, `@actor`, and `^task` completions still work identically there.
-- [ ] When `availableCommands` is empty (a model that advertises no commands, or before the first `available_commands_update`), typing `/` produces no menu and Enter submits normally.

-
-## Tests

Run `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test ai-prompt-composer` and expect green.
-- [ ] Add an `ai-panel` integration test (extend `ai-panel.test.tsx` or `ai-panel-container.test.tsx`) that pushes a fake `available_commands_update` through the conversation store and asserts the composer's autocomplete fires for `/`. Run `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test ai-panel` and expect green.
-- [ ] Full UI suite: `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test` runs green.
-
-## Workflow
-
-- Use `/tdd` â€” write failing tests first (the autocomplete-yield behavior on Enter is the most important regression guard), then implement to make them pass.

--- original
+++ modified
@@ -13,46 +13,47 @@

 ### Approach

-1. **Extend the shared mention infrastructure to support a slash-command flavor**, in `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/cm-mention-autocomplete.ts`. The existing `createMentionCompletionSource(prefix, search)` already accepts any prefix string and a sync or async search function â€” slash commands are just `prefix = "/"` with a sync search over the live `availableCommands` array. The pieces that **don't** transfer cleanly are the entity-shaped `MentionSearchResult` (`{ slug, displayName, color }`) and the colored-dot `info()` widget â€” commands carry a `description` instead of a color. Add a second result shape or extend the existing one so a command result can supply a `description` and skip the color dot. Keep one shared source builder; do not fork into a parallel implementation.
+1. **Extend the shared mention infrastructure to support a slash-command flavor**, in `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/cm-mention-autocomplete.ts`. Done: added `CommandSearchResult` (`{ name, description }`), a `CompletionSearchResult` union + `isCommandResult` guard, command-flavored `buildCommandOption` (label/apply `/<name>`, description in `info()`, no color dot), and an `openOnBarePrefix` option so a bare `/` lists every command. Empty results now yield `null` (closes the menu). One shared source builder â€” no parallel implementation.

-2. **Add a `useCommandCompletionExtension` hook** (or extend the existing `useMentionExtensions` hook in `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/hooks/use-mention-extensions.ts`) that takes an `AvailableCommand[]` (from `@agentclientprotocol/sdk`) and returns the CM6 extension assembled via the same shared `createMentionAutocomplete(sources)` helper. The hook lives in `hooks/` so it has the right React boundary, but the heavy lifting stays in `lib/cm-mention-autocomplete.ts`.
+2. **Add a `useCommandCompletionExtension` hook.** Done: lives in a dedicated lightweight `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/hooks/use-command-completion.ts` (reads no React context, so the composer needn't transitively import the heavy entity-store / window-container chain that `use-mention-extensions.ts` pulls in). Heavy lifting stays in `lib/cm-mention-autocomplete.ts` via `createMentionCompletionSource` + `createMentionAutocomplete`.

-3. **Wire the new extension into the composer.** `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-prompt-composer.tsx`:
-   - Accept the `availableCommands: AvailableCommand[]` list as a new prop on `AiPromptComposerProps` (defaulting to `[]` so existing tests stay green).
-   - Build the autocomplete extension via the new hook and append it to `baseExtensions`.
-   - **Re-introduce the autocomplete-yield guard on the Enter-submit binding** that the composer's docstring already warns about: import `completionStatus` from `@codemirror/autocomplete` and have `buildEnterSubmitExtension`'s `run` return `false` when `completionStatus(view.state) === "active"`, so Enter accepts the highlighted completion instead of submitting. Update the docstring to reflect that autocomplete is now present.
+3. **Wire the new extension into the composer.** Done: `availableCommands` prop (defaults `[]`); extension appended to `baseExtensions`; the Enter-submit binding now imports `completionStatus` and returns `false` when `completionStatus(view.state) === "active"` so Enter accepts the highlighted completion. Docstring updated.

-4. **Thread `availableCommands` from the conversation to the composer.** In `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-panel.tsx`, `AiPanelConversation` already destructures the live `useConversation` result; pull `state.availableCommands` and pass it down through `ComposerArea` to `AiPromptComposer`. `ComposerArea` is in the same file â€” add the prop pass-through there too.
+4. **Thread `availableCommands` from the conversation to the composer.** Done: `AiPanelConversation` pulls `state.availableCommands` and passes it through `ComposerArea` to `AiPromptComposer`.

 ### Non-goals

-- Do **not** intercept slash commands locally or invoke them through any client-side router. Submitting `/plan` should still send the literal text `/plan` to the agent via `sendPrompt`, exactly as it does today â€” the agent owns command execution. This task is purely about discoverability via autocomplete.
-- Do **not** add a color dot, color picker, or any other mention-specific chrome to command completions. The completion menu for `/` should show the command name and its description; the existing `info()` callback shape is enough to render a description without a color dot.
-- Do not change the `#`/`@`/`^` mention sources or their behavior.
+- Do **not** intercept slash commands locally. Honored â€” `apply` inserts the literal `/<name>` and `sendPrompt` sends it verbatim.
+- Do **not** add a color dot or other mention chrome to command completions. Honored â€” command `info()` is plain description text.
+- Do not change the `#`/`@`/`^` mention sources or their behavior. Honored â€” bare-prefix suppression stays the default; `openOnBarePrefix` is opt-in for `/` only.
+
+### Note on the Enter guard

+The task suggested `=== "active"`; the existing `cm-submit-cancel.ts` helper uses `!== null`. We deliberately used the task's `=== "active"`: `activateOnTyping` transiently reports `"pending"` while debouncing over ordinary prose, and a chat composer must still submit on Enter in that window. Only an actually-open menu yields.
+
 ### Why this fits the existing pattern

-`createMentionCompletionSource` is already prefix-parameterized and accepts a sync search function (the `MentionSearchSync` type) â€” the slash-command source is the smallest possible extension of the existing component, not a new component. `createMentionAutocomplete(sources)` already collapses multiple sources into one `autocompletion()` call to avoid the "Config merge conflict for field override" error, so adding a `/` source to the same array stays one autocompletion extension. Reusing the shared component is what keeps the composer's editor, the filter editor, and the markdown field editor on one CM6 autocomplete primitive.
+`createMentionCompletionSource` is prefix-parameterized and accepts a sync search â€” the slash source is the smallest extension of it. `createMentionAutocomplete(sources)` still collapses to one `autocompletion()`. Reuse keeps the composer, filter editor, and markdown field editor on one CM6 autocomplete primitive.

 ## Acceptance Criteria

-- [ ] Typing `/` (anywhere in the composer) opens a completion menu listing every entry in the current `availableCommands` from the live ACP conversation; typing additional characters filters by name prefix/substring (mirroring the existing mention autocomplete filter behavior).
-- [ ] Each completion shows the command `name` (with the `/` prefix) and its `description` in the info area. No color dot is rendered for slash completions.
-- [ ] Accepting a completion (Enter or click) inserts the literal text `/<name>` into the buffer. The composer does NOT intercept or transform the command â€” `sendPrompt` is called with the literal text on the next plain Enter.
-- [ ] When the completion menu is open, plain Enter accepts the highlighted completion and does NOT submit the buffer. With the menu closed, plain Enter still submits per the existing policy. Shift-Enter still inserts a newline regardless of menu state.
-- [ ] Escape with the menu open closes the menu (CM6 default); Escape with the menu closed still drills out via the existing `buildSubmitCancelExtensions` path.
-- [ ] No regressions to the filter editor or markdown field editor: `#tag`, `@actor`, and `^task` completions still work identically there.
-- [ ] When `availableCommands` is empty (a model that advertises no commands, or before the first `available_commands_update`), typing `/` produces no menu and Enter submits normally.
-- [ ] The shared `createMentionCompletionSource` / `createMentionAutocomplete` helpers remain the single completion-source assembly point in `lib/cm-mention-autocomplete.ts`; slash support is added by extending result-shape handling, not by forking into a parallel `cm-slash-autocomplete.ts`.

-- [ ] Extend `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/cm-mention-autocomplete.test.ts` with cases for the `/` prefix: an empty `availableCommands` array yields `null`, a populated one yields a `CompletionResult` whose options' `label` is `/${name}`, `apply` is `/${name}`, and `info()` renders the description text. Run `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test cm-mention-autocomplete` and expect green.

Run `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test ai-prompt-composer` and expect green.
-- [ ] Add an `ai-panel` integration test (extend `ai-panel.test.tsx` or `ai-panel-container.test.tsx`) that pushes a fake `available_commands_update` through the conversation store and asserts the composer's autocomplete fires for `/`. Run `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test ai-panel` and expect green.
-- [ ] Full UI suite: `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test` runs green.
+- [x] Extended `cm-mention-autocomplete.test.ts` with `/` prefix cases: empty search yields `null`; a populated one yields a `CompletionResult` with `label`/`apply` = `/${name}` and `info()` rendering the description (no dot); plus `openOnBarePrefix` auto-trigger and the unchanged default bare-prefix suppression. Green.

reverse_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -13,47 +13,46 @@

 ### Approach

-1. **Extend the shared mention infrastructure to support a slash-command flavor**, in `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/cm-mention-autocomplete.ts`. Done: added `CommandSearchResult` (`{ name, description }`), a `CompletionSearchResult` union + `isCommandResult` guard, command-flavored `buildCommandOption` (label/apply `/<name>`, description in `info()`, no color dot), and an `openOnBarePrefix` option so a bare `/` lists every command. Empty results now yield `null` (closes the menu). One shared source builder â€” no parallel implementation.
+1. **Extend the shared mention infrastructure to support a slash-command flavor**, in `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/cm-mention-autocomplete.ts`. The existing `createMentionCompletionSource(prefix, search)` already accepts any prefix string and a sync or async search function â€” slash commands are just `prefix = "/"` with a sync search over the live `availableCommands` array. The pieces that **don't** transfer cleanly are the entity-shaped `MentionSearchResult` (`{ slug, displayName, color }`) and the colored-dot `info()` widget â€” commands carry a `description` instead of a color. Add a second result shape or extend the existing one so a command result can supply a `description` and skip the color dot. Keep one shared source builder; do not fork into a parallel implementation.

-2. **Add a `useCommandCompletionExtension` hook.** Done: lives in a dedicated lightweight `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/hooks/use-command-completion.ts` (reads no React context, so the composer needn't transitively import the heavy entity-store / window-container chain that `use-mention-extensions.ts` pulls in). Heavy lifting stays in `lib/cm-mention-autocomplete.ts` via `createMentionCompletionSource` + `createMentionAutocomplete`.
+2. **Add a `useCommandCompletionExtension` hook** (or extend the existing `useMentionExtensions` hook in `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/hooks/use-mention-extensions.ts`) that takes an `AvailableCommand[]` (from `@agentclientprotocol/sdk`) and returns the CM6 extension assembled via the same shared `createMentionAutocomplete(sources)` helper. The hook lives in `hooks/` so it has the right React boundary, but the heavy lifting stays in `lib/cm-mention-autocomplete.ts`.

-3. **Wire the new extension into the composer.** Done: `availableCommands` prop (defaults `[]`); extension appended to `baseExtensions`; the Enter-submit binding now imports `completionStatus` and returns `false` when `completionStatus(view.state) === "active"` so Enter accepts the highlighted completion. Docstring updated.
+3. **Wire the new extension into the composer.** `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-prompt-composer.tsx`:
+   - Accept the `availableCommands: AvailableCommand[]` list as a new prop on `AiPromptComposerProps` (defaulting to `[]` so existing tests stay green).
+   - Build the autocomplete extension via the new hook and append it to `baseExtensions`.
+   - **Re-introduce the autocomplete-yield guard on the Enter-submit binding** that the composer's docstring already warns about: import `completionStatus` from `@codemirror/autocomplete` and have `buildEnterSubmitExtension`'s `run` return `false` when `completionStatus(view.state) === "active"`, so Enter accepts the highlighted completion instead of submitting. Update the docstring to reflect that autocomplete is now present.

-4. **Thread `availableCommands` from the conversation to the composer.** Done: `AiPanelConversation` pulls `state.availableCommands` and passes it through `ComposerArea` to `AiPromptComposer`.
+4. **Thread `availableCommands` from the conversation to the composer.** In `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-panel.tsx`, `AiPanelConversation` already destructures the live `useConversation` result; pull `state.availableCommands` and pass it down through `ComposerArea` to `AiPromptComposer`. `ComposerArea` is in the same file â€” add the prop pass-through there too.

 ### Non-goals

-- Do **not** intercept slash commands locally. Honored â€” `apply` inserts the literal `/<name>` and `sendPrompt` sends it verbatim.
-- Do **not** add a color dot or other mention chrome to command completions. Honored â€” command `info()` is plain description text.
-- Do not change the `#`/`@`/`^` mention sources or their behavior. Honored â€” bare-prefix suppression stays the default; `openOnBarePrefix` is opt-in for `/` only.
-
-### Note on the Enter guard
-
-The task suggested `=== "active"`; the existing `cm-submit-cancel.ts` helper uses `!== null`. We deliberately used the task's `=== "active"`: `activateOnTyping` transiently reports `"pending"` while debouncing over ordinary prose, and a chat composer must still submit on Enter in that window. Only an actually-open menu yields.
+- Do **not** intercept slash commands locally or invoke them through any client-side router. Submitting `/plan` should still send the literal text `/plan` to the agent via `sendPrompt`, exactly as it does today â€” the agent owns command execution. This task is purely about discoverability via autocomplete.
+- Do **not** add a color dot, color picker, or any other mention-specific chrome to command completions. The completion menu for `/` should show the command name and its description; the existing `info()` callback shape is enough to render a description without a color dot.
+- Do not change the `#`/`@`/`^` mention sources or their behavior.

 ### Why this fits the existing pattern

-`createMentionCompletionSource` is prefix-parameterized and accepts a sync search â€” the slash source is the smallest extension of it. `createMentionAutocomplete(sources)` still collapses to one `autocompletion()`. Reuse keeps the composer, filter editor, and markdown field editor on one CM6 autocomplete primitive.
+`createMentionCompletionSource` is already prefix-parameterized and accepts a sync search function (the `MentionSearchSync` type) â€” the slash-command source is the smallest possible extension of the existing component, not a new component. `createMentionAutocomplete(sources)` already collapses multiple sources into one `autocompletion()` call to avoid the "Config merge conflict for field override" error, so adding a `/` source to the same array stays one autocompletion extension. Reusing the shared component is what keeps the composer's editor, the filter editor, and the markdown field editor on one CM6 autocomplete primitive.

 ## Acceptance Criteria

-- [x] Typing `/` (anywhere in the composer) opens a completion menu listing every entry in the current `availableCommands`; typing additional characters filters by name substring.
-- [x] Each completion shows the command `name` (with the `/` prefix) and its `description` in the info area. No color dot is rendered for slash completions.
-- [x] Accepting a completion (Enter or click) inserts the literal text `/<name>` into the buffer. The composer does NOT intercept or transform the command.
-- [x] When the completion menu is open, plain Enter accepts the highlighted completion and does NOT submit. With the menu closed, plain Enter still submits. Shift-Enter still inserts a newline regardless of menu state.
-- [x] Escape with the menu open closes the menu (CM6 default); Escape with the menu closed still drills out via the existing `buildSubmitCancelExtensions` path.
-- [x] No regressions to the filter editor or markdown field editor: `#tag`, `@actor`, and `^task` completions still work identically there.
-- [x] When `availableCommands` is empty, typing `/` produces no menu and Enter submits normally.
-- [x] The shared `createMentionCompletionSource` / `createMentionAutocomplete` helpers remain the single completion-source assembly point; slash support is added by extending result-shape handling, not by forking into a parallel file.
+- [ ] Typing `/` (anywhere in the composer) opens a completion menu listing every entry in the current `availableCommands` from the live ACP conversation; typing additional characters filters by name prefix/substring (mirroring the existing mention autocomplete filter behavior).
+- [ ] Each completion shows the command `name` (with the `/` prefix) and its `description` in the info area. No color dot is rendered for slash completions.
+- [ ] Accepting a completion (Enter or click) inserts the literal text `/<name>` into the buffer. The composer does NOT intercept or transform the command â€” `sendPrompt` is called with the literal text on the next plain Enter.
+- [ ] When the completion menu is open, plain Enter accepts the highlighted completion and does NOT submit the buffer. With the menu closed, plain Enter still submits per the existing policy. Shift-Enter still inserts a newline regardless of menu state.
+- [ ] Escape with the menu open closes the menu (CM6 default); Escape with the menu closed still drills out via the existing `buildSubmitCancelExtensions` path.
+- [ ] No regressions to the filter editor or markdown field editor: `#tag`, `@actor`, and `^task` completions still work identically there.
+- [ ] When `availableCommands` is empty (a model that advertises no commands, or before the first `available_commands_update`), typing `/` produces no menu and Enter submits normally.
+- [ ] The shared `createMentionCompletionSource` / `createMentionAutocomplete` helpers remain the single completion-source assembly point in `lib/cm-mention-autocomplete.ts`; slash support is added by extending result-shape handling, not by forking into a parallel `cm-slash-autocomplete.ts`.

 ## Tests

Green.

-- [x] Extended `ai-prompt-composer.test.tsx`: typing `/` opens the menu when non-empty and not when empty; plain Enter with the menu open accepts (asserts buffer `/plan`) and does not submit; plain Enter with the menu closed submits; Shift-Enter inserts a newline. Green.
-- [x] Added an `ai-panel.test.tsx` integration test pushing a fake `available_commands_update` through the conversation store and asserting the composer's `/` autocomplete fires. Green.
-- [x] Full UI suite: `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test` runs green (254 files, 2427 tests; `tsc --noEmit` clean).

Run `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test cm-mention-autocomplete` and expect green.
+- [ ] Add a unit test in `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/hooks/__tests__/use-mention-extensions.test.ts` (or a sibling test file for the new hook) that mounts the hook with a fixture `AvailableCommand[]` and asserts the returned extension array contains a single `autocompletion` config wired to the `/` source plus the existing mention sources.

Run `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test ai-prompt-composer` and expect green.
+- [ ] Add an `ai-panel` integration test (extend `ai-panel.test.tsx` or `ai-panel-container.test.tsx`) that pushes a fake `available_commands_update` through the conversation store and asserts the composer's autocomplete fires for `/`. Run `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test ai-panel` and expect green.
+- [ ] Full UI suite: `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test` runs green.

-- [x] Typing `/` (anywhere in the composer) opens a completion menu listing every entry in the current `availableCommands`; typing additional characters filters by name substring.
-- [x] Each completion shows the command `name` (with the `/` prefix) and its `description` in the info area. No color dot is rendered for slash completions.
-- [x] Accepting a completion (Enter or click) inserts the literal text `/<name>` into the buffer. The composer does NOT intercept or transform the command.
-- [x] When the completion menu is open, plain Enter accepts the highlighted completion and does NOT submit. With the menu closed, plain Enter still submits. Shift-Enter still inserts a newline regardless of menu state.
-- [x] Escape with the menu open closes the menu (CM6 default); Escape with the menu closed still drills out via the existing `buildSubmitCancelExtensions` path.

-- [x] When `availableCommands` is empty, typing `/` produces no menu and Enter submits normally.
-- [x] The shared `createMentionCompletionSource` / `createMentionAutocomplete` helpers remain the single completion-source assembly point; slash support is added by extending result-shape handling, not by forking into a parallel file.
+Manual test surfaced that `/` showed nothing on a fresh chat. Root cause: the menu is driven by the live `availableCommands`, which the claude backend forwards (from the CLI init message) only when the session starts â€” and the session started lazily on the *first* `sendPrompt`. So a never-sent-to conversation had an empty command list.

-## Tests
+Fix (chosen direction: **warm up on model select**):
+- [x] Added `warmUp()` to the conversation API (`conversation.ts`): fire-and-forget eager session start; idempotent; shares one in-flight session with `sendPrompt` via a new `sessionPromiseRef` guard so a warm-up racing the first send never spawns two agents. `newConversation` clears the in-flight ref.
+- [x] `AiPanelConversation` warms up via `useEffect` whenever `modelReady && messages.length === 0` â€” covers model-select mount AND re-warms after `ai.newChat` so `/` keeps working in a fresh chat.
+- [x] Tests: `conversation.test.tsx` (warmUp folds init `available_commands_update` without a prompt; warmUp racing sendPrompt â†’ one session); `ai-panel.test.tsx` (`/` works before any message; re-warms after `ai.newChat`). Init-time ambient updates added to both test harnesses' `startSession` to mirror the real agent.
+- [x] Full UI suite green: 254 files, 2431 tests; `tsc --noEmit` clean.

Green.
-- [x] Added `hooks/__tests__/use-command-completion.test.ts` mounting the hook with a fixture `AvailableCommand[]` and asserting the `/` menu opens, lists commands, and filters by substring; empty list returns no extension. Green.
-- [x] Extended `ai-prompt-composer.test.tsx`: typing `/` opens the menu when non-empty and not when empty; plain Enter with the menu open accepts (asserts buffer `/plan`) and does not submit; plain Enter with the menu closed submits; Shift-Enter inserts a newline. Green.
-- [x] Added an `ai-panel.test.tsx` integration test pushing a fake `available_commands_update` through the conversation store and asserting the composer's `/` autocomplete fires. Green.
-- [x] Full UI suite: `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test` runs green (254 files, 2427 tests; `tsc --noEmit` clean).
+Note: a local-llama model advertises no commands (`agent_trait_impl.rs`), so `/` is correctly empty there.

 ## Workflow

-- Used `/tdd` â€” failing test first, watched RED, implemented to GREEN, refactored, for each of the five layers.
\ No newline at end of file
+- `/tdd` throughout â€” failing test first, watched RED, GREEN, refactor â€” for every layer including the warm-up follow-up.
\ No newline at end of file

+2. **Add a `useCommandCompletionExtension` hook.** Done: lives in a dedicated lightweight `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/hooks/use-command-completion.ts` (reads no React context, so the composer needn't transitively import the heavy entity-store / window-container chain that `use-mention-extensions.ts` pulls in). Heavy lifting stays in `lib/cm-mention-autocomplete.ts` via `createMentionCompletionSource` + `createMentionAutocomplete`.

-## Acceptance Criteria
+3. **Wire the new extension into the composer.** Done: `availableCommands` prop (defaults `[]`); extension appended to `baseExtensions`; the Enter-submit binding now imports `completionStatus` and returns `false` when `completionStatus(view.state) === "active"` so Enter accepts the highlighted completion. Docstring updated.

-- [x] Typing `/` opens a completion menu listing every `availableCommands` entry; typing more filters by substring.
-- [x] Each completion shows `/<name>` and its `description` in the info area. No color dot.
-- [x] Accepting (Enter or click) inserts literal `/<name>`. No interception/transform.
-- [x] Menu open: plain Enter accepts and does NOT submit. Menu closed: plain Enter submits. Shift-Enter always inserts a newline.
-- [x] Escape open â†’ closes menu (CM6); Escape closed â†’ drills out via `buildSubmitCancelExtensions`.
-- [x] No regressions to `#tag`/`@actor`/`^task` completions in the filter/markdown editors.
-- [x] Empty `availableCommands` â†’ typing `/` produces no menu, Enter submits.
-- [x] Shared helpers remain the single completion-source assembly point; no parallel file.
+4. **Thread `availableCommands` from the conversation to the composer.** Done: `AiPanelConversation` pulls `state.availableCommands` and passes it through `ComposerArea` to `AiPromptComposer`.

-## Tests
+### Non-goals

-- [x] `cm-mention-autocomplete.test.ts`: `/` prefix cases (emptyâ†’null; label/apply `/${name}`; `info()` description, no dot; `openOnBarePrefix` auto-trigger; default bare-prefix suppression unchanged).
-- [x] `hooks/__tests__/use-command-completion.test.ts`: hook opens/lists/filters the `/` menu; empty list â†’ no extension.
-- [x] `ai-prompt-composer.test.tsx`: `/` opens menu when non-empty, not when empty; Enter-with-menu accepts (buffer `/plan`) and doesn't submit; Enter-menu-closed submits; Shift-Enter newline.
-- [x] `ai-panel.test.tsx`: streamed `available_commands_update` drives the composer's `/` menu.
-- [x] Full UI suite green.
+- Do **not** intercept slash commands locally. Honored â€” `apply` inserts the literal `/<name>` and `sendPrompt` sends it verbatim.
+- Do **not** add a color dot or other mention chrome to command completions. Honored â€” command `info()` is plain description text.
+- Do not change the `#`/`@`/`^` mention sources or their behavior. Honored â€” bare-prefix suppression stays the default; `openOnBarePrefix` is opt-in for `/` only.

-## Follow-up â€” manual verification fix (warm-up on model select)
+### Note on the Enter guard

-Manual test surfaced that `/` showed nothing on a fresh chat. Root cause: the menu is driven by the live `availableCommands`, which the claude backend forwards (from the CLI init message) only when the session starts â€” and the session started lazily on the *first* `sendPrompt`. So a never-sent-to conversation had an empty command list.
+The task suggested `=== "active"`; the existing `cm-submit-cancel.ts` helper uses `!== null`. We deliberately used the task's `=== "active"`: `activateOnTyping` transiently reports `"pending"` while debouncing over ordinary prose, and a chat composer must still submit on Enter in that window. Only an actually-open menu yields.
+
+### Why this fits the existing pattern

-Fix (chosen direction: **warm up on model select**):
-- [x] Added `warmUp()` to the conversation API (`conversation.ts`): fire-and-forget eager session start; idempotent; shares one in-flight session with `sendPrompt` via a new `sessionPromiseRef` guard so a warm-up racing the first send never spawns two agents. `newConversation` clears the in-flight ref.
-- [x] `AiPanelConversation` warms up via `useEffect` whenever `modelReady && messages.length === 0` â€” covers model-select mount AND re-warms after `ai.newChat` so `/` keeps working in a fresh chat.
-- [x] Tests: `conversation.test.tsx` (warmUp folds init `available_commands_update` without a prompt; warmUp racing sendPrompt â†’ one session); `ai-panel.test.tsx` (`/` works before any message; re-warms after `ai.newChat`). Init-time ambient updates added to both test harnesses' `startSession` to mirror the real agent.
-- [x] Full UI suite green: 254 files, 2431 tests; `tsc --noEmit` clean.
+`createMentionCompletionSource` is prefix-parameterized and accepts a sync search â€” the slash source is the smallest extension of it. `createMentionAutocomplete(sources)` still collapses to one `autocompletion()`. Reuse keeps the composer, filter editor, and markdown field editor on one CM6 autocomplete primitive.

-Note: a local-llama model advertises no commands (`agent_trait_impl.rs`), so `/` is correctly empty there.
+## Acceptance Criteria

+- [x] Typing `/` (anywhere in the composer) opens a completion menu listing every entry in the current `availableCommands`; typing additional characters filters by name substring.
+- [x] Each completion shows the command `name` (with the `/` prefix) and its `description` in the info area. No color dot is rendered for slash completions.
+- [x] Accepting a completion (Enter or click) inserts the literal text `/<name>` into the buffer. The composer does NOT intercept or transform the command.
+- [x] When the completion menu is open, plain Enter accepts the highlighted completion and does NOT submit. With the menu closed, plain Enter still submits. Shift-Enter still inserts a newline regardless of menu state.
+- [x] Escape with the menu open closes the menu (CM6 default); Escape with the menu closed still drills out via the existing `buildSubmitCancelExtensions` path.
+- [x] No regressions to the filter editor or markdown field editor: `#tag`, `@actor`, and `^task` completions still work identically there.
+- [x] When `availableCommands` is empty, typing `/` produces no menu and Enter submits normally.
+- [x] The shared `createMentionCompletionSource` / `createMentionAutocomplete` helpers remain the single completion-source assembly point; slash support is added by extending result-shape handling, not by forking into a parallel file.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [x] Extended `cm-mention-autocomplete.test.ts` with `/` prefix cases: empty search yields `null`; a populated one yields a `CompletionResult` with `label`/`apply` = `/${name}` and `info()` rendering the description (no dot); plus `openOnBarePrefix` auto-trigger and the unchanged default bare-prefix suppression. Green.
+- [x] Added `hooks/__tests__/use-command-completion.test.ts` mounting the hook with a fixture `AvailableCommand[]` and asserting the `/` menu opens, lists commands, and filters by substring; empty list returns no extension. Green.
+- [x] Extended `ai-prompt-composer.test.tsx`: typing `/` opens the menu when non-empty and not when empty; plain Enter with the menu open accepts (asserts buffer `/plan`) and does not submit; plain Enter with the menu closed submits; Shift-Enter inserts a newline. Green.
+- [x] Added an `ai-panel.test.tsx` integration test pushing a fake `available_commands_update` through the conversation store and asserting the composer's `/` autocomplete fires. Green.
+- [x] Full UI suite: `pnpm --filter ./apps/kanban-app/ui test` runs green (254 files, 2427 tests; `tsc --noEmit` clean).
+
 ## Workflow

-- `/tdd` throughout â€” failing test first, watched RED, GREEN, refactor â€” for every layer including the warm-up follow-up.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Used `/tdd` â€” failing test first, watched RED, implemented to GREEN, refactored, for each of the five layers.
\ No newline at end of file

+## Review Findings (2026-05-23 10:34)
+
+Task-mode review of commit `b792ceec8` (reviewer subagent, layered + JS/TS guidelines). 0 blockers, 1 warning, 2 nits â€” all resolved in commit `a31efde5`.
+
+### Warnings
+- [x] `conversation.ts` `ensureSession` â€” a `newConversation` (ai.newChat) firing while a warm-up's `startSession` was still in flight let the abandoned start write its session back into `sessionRef`, so a fresh chat silently reused the prior ACP session (breaks brand-new-session-per-chat). Fixed: a `sessionGenerationRef` bumped on `newConversation`; a start refuses to cache its session if the generation changed; in-flight-promise cleanup guarded so an abandoned start can't clear a newer start's slot. Regression test added (`newConversation during an in-flight warm-up does not cache the abandoned session`).
+
+### Nits
+- [x] `hooks/use-command-completion.ts` â€” renamed single-letter `q` â†’ `lowerQuery` per the JS/TS no-abbreviations guideline.
+- [x] `lib/cm-mention-autocomplete.ts` `isCommandResult` â€” declined: the `"name" in result` structural narrowing is correct for this 2-member union; an explicit `kind` discriminant would be speculative over-engineering. Noted for if the union grows.
\ No newline at end of file
